title: Greeting Each Argument
prompt_md:
## Goal

A real CLI greets every name passed after the program name on the command line. Build that logic here. Because the sandbox cannot pass real `argv`, the arguments arrive on stdin instead -- treat each one exactly as you would an entry of `argv`.

For every name, print a greeting. If there are no names at all, say so instead.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `N` (the number of name arguments, like `argc - 1`).
- The next `N` lines: one name each (names may contain spaces).

## Output

- For each name, in order: `Hello, <name>!`
- If `N` is 0: print exactly `No names provided.`

title: Numeric Arguments
prompt_md:
## Goal

Command line arguments always arrive as strings, even when they represent numbers, so a CLI must convert them before doing math. Simulate that: read several string arguments, convert each to an integer, and report two things about the collection -- the total and the largest value.

Because the sandbox cannot pass real `argv`, the arguments arrive on stdin. You are guaranteed at least one argument.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `N` (1 <= N <= 100), the count of numeric arguments.
- The next `N` lines: one integer (as text) each. Values may be negative.

## Output

Exactly two lines:
```
Sum: <total>
Max: <largest>
```
